evidence_quotes.quote: Perhaps this is addressed in the "unfair personalisation practices" item but I unfortunately doubt it.
loot box style games or social media slot machine design.
The ability to "turn off/delete/remove" your product that you purchased, making it inaccessible on demand.
A company should not own a killswitch to a product that I purchase.
Technofeudalism (coined by Yanis Varoufakis) - Another tricky one to solve but for a different reason.

paragraphs.text: First of all, thank you for this. We need more laws to keep up with the fast evolving digital age. Each of the items listed seem to be pointing in the right direction and it is great to see some forward thinkers in charge of new legislation. When it comes to the digitalization of our modern society I have five main concerns: 1 - Privacy - I have serious concerns about where non-EU countries are moving with privacy. The argument is always about protecting children but putting everyone's data in a single place is prone to exploitation. With AI gaining the ability to agregate and misuse this data the chances of your information being exploited is only increasing with the use of generated images and videos. Do you like the idea of scammers pretending to be you now with a video call with your face and voice? Having a forced digital persona is only a good idea until a data leak occurs and time and time again, this always happens. I dont trust my biometric digital data in the hands of any government much less so in any company's database. I think we really need to crack down on companies abusing their reach when it comes to data protection. Perhaps this is addressed in the "unfair personalisation practices" item but I unfortunately doubt it. I would love to see more privacy protections. Companies need you to "opt in" to data collection, not the other way around. Companies need to see serious consequences when caught collecting your personal data without your consent and data brokering should be illegal. For me this is my main concern today. 2 - Digital killswitch - I have concerns with is the misuse of digital ownership. This is happening in gaming companies currently but other industries are learning from and adapting it. The ability to "turn off/delete/remove" your product that you purchased, making it inaccessible on demand. A company should not own a killswitch to a product that I purchase. Acursed farms from stop killing games has some videos about this but essentially it has to do with current video games being turned off after being purchased. Some people will spend thousands on in-game purchases which can then be turned off once the company no longer deems it profitable/desireable. This problem however is not limmited to video games and is only growing. I believe there is no item yet in this initiative that addressis this issue. 3 - Digital gambling/slots - I believe that the "addictive design of digital products" item could already address this but I will add my two cents here briefly. Social media/games/streaming platforms try to optimize time spent on their platform as it often correlates to money earned. Gambling with real money is fairly well regulated but there is no regulation for loot box style games or social media slot machine design. 4 - Digital addiction in young children - I am referring to excessive gaming or social media usage with children under 18. This one is a bit tricky because solving this issue should NOT be at the cost of everyone's privacy. Do not let citizens of europe pay for the sins of the parents. Hopefully the "addictive design of digital products" item addresses this 5 - Technofeudalism (coined by Yanis Varoufakis) - Another tricky one to solve but for a different reason. The responsible culprits seem to have too much power. I am not sure how to address this properly and understand it will take a big push but I am referring to the ability for a platform to act as not only a monopoly but essentially a feudal lord where they have absolute control over a store owner. They decide who does/does not appear on their platform and can extract exhuberent value from the store owner (Think amazon market with their sellers, Uber with their drivers etc.) Thank you for the initiative. I think we are headed in the right direction with Europe when I look at how the other countries act. I have never been more proud to be a european than today. Protect the people.
